scope_note: QA Mod-24 Quadrance 2-adic Signature cert [287]. Primary sources: Wildberger (2005) Divine Proportions Wild Egg Books ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8 Ch1 quadrance G=b^2+e^2; Wall (1960) Fibonacci series modulo m Amer. Math. Monthly 67(6) DOI 10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 orbit periods. Mechanism: cert [279] (Orbit Access Theorem); cert [283] (RT Quadrance v3 signature for mod-9). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): for (b,e) in {1,...,24}^2, v2(b^2+e^2) = 2*min(v2(b),v2(e)) + delta where delta=1 if v2(b)=v2(e) else 0. Equivalently: v2(G) >=6 iff satellite or singularity (orbit class determined by 8|b AND 8|e); cosmos always has v2(G)<=5. The delta=1 term arises because odd squares satisfy x^2=1 mod 8, so their sum =2 mod 8 gives one extra factor of 2. This CONTRASTS with mod-9 cert [283] where the 3-adic formula v3(G)=2*v3(gcd(b,e)) has NO delta because nonzero mod-3 residues square to 1 and 1+1=2 which is coprime to 3. Verified exhaustively all 576 pairs. Theorem NT: G=b*b+e*e is integer arithmetic; v2 is 2-adic valuation (integer); orbit_family on (b,e,24) is integer classifier; no float feedback into QA layer. Lineage: derived 2026-05-31 to characterize the prime-specific boundary of the v3(G)=2*v3(gcd) formula from cert [283].
